title: “Aliens” Drove This Man Insane! (Ft. Greg Bishop)
summary: Greg Bishop discusses his book Project Beta, which documents how Paul Benowitz, an electronics engineer living near Kirtland Air Force Base, was driven to mental instability by Air Force Office of Special Investigations (AFOSI) counter-intelligence operations. The interview explores how intelligence agencies used UFO beliefs as tools for counter-intelligence, feeding Benowitz false information about alien signals and underground bases while monitoring his activities and contacts.

acronyms.expansion: Air Force Office of Special Investigations
acronyms.definition: Air Force counter-intelligence agency involved in security operations at Kirtland Air Force Base
A UFO research group based in Arizona, run by Jim and Coral Lorenzen, that split off from the Midwest UFO Network
UFO research organization, formerly known as the Midwest UFO Network
World War II intelligence agency, predecessor to the CIA
Display technology used in old TV screens and oscilloscopes

organizations_mentioned: Air Force Office of Special Investigations

events.description: Paul Benowitz begins seeing lights/orbs flying over Kirtland Air Force Base
Mario Woods witnesses UFO and beings at Ellsworth Air Force Base, debriefed by Rick Dodie
Bill Moore co-writes The Roswell Incident with Stanton Friedman, bringing Roswell crash to public attention
Operation Upshot Knothole nuclear test at Nevada Test Range, associated with Kingman crash claims
Don Carter at Kirtland Air Force Base convening interdisciplinary meetings with Henry Kissinger present

key_claims.claim: Intelligence agencies used UFOs as a 'Swiss Army knife' tool for counter-intelligence operations
Paul Benowitz was already showing signs of instability before AFOSI operations began, but intelligence agents encouraged and worsened his condition
J. Allen Hynek delivered a computer to Benowitz that was programmed to feed him false alien signals and imagery
The Air Force flew Benowitz to Archuleta Mesa and placed props including jeeps and water tanks to misdirect his attention from Kirtland
Harry Rositzki, a retired CIA Soviet operations expert, was pulled out of retirement to run the unofficial counter-intelligence operation that included the Benowitz affair
Bill Moore was recruited by intelligence because he correctly identified planted false documents as fabrications
NSA personnel may have been stationed across the street from Benowitz to beam false signals into his equipment
Prosaic technology is often used as a cover for UFO phenomena, not the reverse
Eric Henry Wang worked on secret UFO-related projects at Kirtland Air Force Base and was managed by Henry Kissinger
Bill Steinman was pushed out of Skunk Works employment because of his UFO research interests
